audit_items.proof_trace: In peci_device_destroy (called from remove_store), device_unregister(&device->dev) at L209 calls put_device which, when the device has only its initial kref=1 (no driver bound, no iteration reference), synchronously calls peci_device_release → kfree(device). The subsequent device->deleted = true at L210 is then a write-after-free. Within remove_store itself no pointer is used after peci_device_destroy returns, but the UAF occurs inside that callee on the path originating from remove_store.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A privileged user writes '1' to /sys/bus/peci/devices/<addr>/remove when the peci_device has no driver bound (kref=1); device_unregister frees the device struct, and the subsequent device->deleted = true writes a freed heap byte, enabling heap-corruption primitives.
